title: cross-bridge anomaly detector — rolling per-bridge response_time + failure_rate comparator with deferred-recommendation INSUFFICIENT_DATA fallback
summary: Build sweeper-safe .hexa analyzer (tool/bridge_anomaly.hexa) that compares each bridge's most recent selftest sample against the median of its prior window. Flag DURATION_SPIKE when current_ms > multiplier * prior_median AND current_ms >= 200ms jitter floor. Flag FRESH_FAILURE when latest status is non-PASS and prior fail_ratio < 0.5. Emit INSUFFICIENT_DATA sentinel when history TSV is missing OR every bridge has < 3 samples (network-3 deferred-recommendation pattern). Wiring bridge_health.sh to APPEND per-bridge rows is intentionally deferred to omega-bridge-15. State stub state/bridge_health_history.tsv created with documented schema header.

predecessors.omega_bridge_13: 16 .hexa callers simplified onto bridge_helper config table
predecessors.omega_network_3: psi_log_analyzer.sh deferred-recommendation pattern (insufficient_data gate at min_count=50)

context.problem: bridge_health.sh emits aggregate counts to state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl. Per-bridge $DUR is computed in-loop (lines 210-217 of bridge_health.sh) and printed to stdout under --quiet=0/--json=0, but is never persisted. Consequence: a bridge that was 100ms yesterday and 5000ms today never trips a flag — the 50× regression is invisible.
context.design_goal: lightweight rolling comparison with two flag rules (duration spike + fresh failure) + INSUFFICIENT_DATA fallback so the tool is safe to invoke from day 1 even with no history.
scope_constraints_honoured: hexa-only-suffix (raw 9): NEW file is .hexa, no .sh edits this cycle

files_created.exec_strategy: single awk invocation per bridge for median + threshold + ratio + flag decisions; no per-element shell-out (avoids the digit-by-digit fan-out that was hanging an earlier draft)

files_created.role: schema stub + canonical default path for analyzer
files_created.note: Until omega-bridge-15 lands, default invocation returns INSUFFICIENT_DATA. Operators may pre-populate or use --history PATH.

sentinel.flagged_row_format: FLAG  <bridge>  DURATION_SPIKE current_ms=X median_ms=Y threshold_ms=Z[; FRESH_FAILURE current_status=S prior_fail_ratio=R]

algorithm.duration_spike: current_dur_ms > multiplier * median(prior_window_durations) AND current_dur_ms >= 200ms (DURATION_FLOOR_MS jitter guard)
algorithm.insufficient_data: history file absent OR every bridge has fewer than 3 samples in the window

actual_output: FLAG  codata  DURATION_SPIKE current_ms=5500 median_ms=102.50 threshold_ms=307.50
fix:    investigate flagged bridge(s); check upstream API latency / outage / cache state
__BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ WARN bridges=5 samples=35 flagged=2 window=7 mult=3.0
__BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ INSUFFICIENT_DATA bridges=0 samples=0 flagged=0 window=7 mult=3.0 reason=history_missing path=/tmp/no_such_history_file.tsv
fix:    seed history TSV via bridge_health.sh wiring (ω-bridge-15) or pass --history PATH

header_only_history_test.fixture: /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_health_history.tsv (only schema comment lines, no data rows)
actual_output: __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ INSUFFICIENT_DATA bridges=0 samples=0 flagged=0 window=7 mult=3.0 reason=no_data_rows path=/Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_health_history.tsv

perf_note: Initial draft used per-digit shell-out (_to_float / _to_int loops) which produced thousands of exec() calls and hung hexa stage1 for >5min on a 35-row fixture. Refactored to one awk invocation per bridge — for the full 16-bridge registry this is ~16 exec() calls, sub-second total. Lesson recorded: hexa stage1 string→number must be batched, never per-character.

pending_followup_omega_bridge_15.scope: wire bridge_health.sh to APPEND a row per bridge per sweep into state/bridge_health_history.tsv
pending_followup_omega_bridge_15.code_locus: tool/bridge_health.sh lines 210-217 (per-bridge loop computes T0/T1/DUR; add `printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' ... >> $HISTORY_TSV`)
considerations: DUR currently in seconds (line 217: DUR=$((T1 - T0))) — multiply by 1000 for ms or update analyzer floor
rotate the history TSV to keep size bounded (e.g. last 500 rows = ~31 sweeps × 16 bridges)
atomic append (>> on POSIX is atomic for <PIPE_BUF byte writes; one row << 4096 bytes)
session class: this cycle is hexa-only-suffix; ω-bridge-15 will need shell-edits suffix

rollback.level_1: rm /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/bridge_anomaly.hexa  # analyzer is purely additive; nothing depends on it yet
rollback.level_2: rm /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_health_history.tsv  # stub schema is comment-only; safe to remove

compliance.hexa_only_suffix: honoured (raw 9): only .hexa + .tsv stub created; no .sh / .py edits
compliance.raw_71_report_only: INSUFFICIENT_DATA path returns 0 (no exit-code escalation); WARN does not exit non-zero (analyzer is informational)
compliance.raw_73_admissibility: two flag rules with explicit thresholds and named sentinels; INSUFFICIENT_DATA carries reason= field
compliance.raw_77_append_only: history TSV format is documented as append-only; analyzer never writes to it

constraints_honoured: Created NEW tool/bridge_anomaly.hexa (.hexa for sweeper safety) — no edits to existing bridges
ZERO bridge_health.sh edits (wiring deferred to omega-bridge-15)
NO live API hammering — synthetic /tmp/fake_bridge_history.tsv only
Stopped when JSON written + synthetic test PASS
verification_summary: Analyzer compiles + executes under hexa stage1. Three test paths exercised: WARN (2/5 flagged on synthetic 35-row fixture, both anomalies caught with correct rule attribution), INSUFFICIENT_DATA on missing file, INSUFFICIENT_DATA on header-only file. Median computation cross-checked: prior=[100,95,110,105,98,120] sorted=[95,98,100,105,110,120], median=(100+105)/2=102.5 — matches awk output 102.50. Threshold at 3x = 307.5 — matches awk output 307.50. FRESH_FAILURE rule fires on arxiv where 6 prior PASS samples → fail_ratio=0.0 < 0.5 with current=FAIL. Per session brief, no further wiring this cycle.
